## [00:00 - 03:02]

You might be shocked to discover that most big brands generate over 30% of their revenue from email. You can see here that from my store, we've done 18 million in the last 12 months and 34% of that revenue has come from emails that we've sent to people on our database. Emails are the best brand asset that you can generate as a business owner. They are better than Facebook fans, Instagram followers and YouTube subscribers combined. Shopify did this rating for Black Friday, Cyber Monday of all the traffic that was going to Shopify stores over the Black Friday, Cyber Monday sale. Email was the highest converting source of traffic. It was higher than direct traffic, which means people like typing in a brand's name and a search engine. So it's really, really powerful. Once someone gets on your email list, you can send them content to warm them up and get them interested in buying from you. You can see here, these are some of the content emails that we send out to the folks who are on our database. Now, every single email that we send makes money. And you can obviously see the ones in here that were sale emails versus the ones that were content emails. But the content emails make the sale emails work in a way because they're keeping people engaged and keeping them active. We were kind of reminded over the last year how important email is as a brand. And at the start of this year, I had one major goal, do whatever it takes to grow my email list. So I know that everyone likes the chance of winning something. And I've seen how well contests work to keep my current email subscribers engaged, right? Where I'll do like a social contest. I'm one of my social channels and say, hey, yeah, I've got a contest going on. You can win something. Works really well with the folks who are on my email list and on my social channels. So I thought to myself, what a contest work for cold traffic. Would it be an effective means of email lead generation for my brand and get people onto my list who'd be interested in my products in the long term? So I decided to test it. And I came up with this funnel right here that I'm about to walk you through. It's not a very complex funnel. It's only a couple pages with some ads, but it's extremely powerful to get people onto my email list and interested in my products. Let me show you how it works. So it starts with ads that go to cold traffic to new people who don't know about you yet. And the ads are saying, hey, we've got this amazing product and you can get it for free by visiting this page. Now someone sees this ad on Facebook and a couple things to note about these ads is you'll notice that we're using a colored backdrop, right? So it's really compelling and the products stick out against the colored backdrop and we're using text overlay, text overlay and square images. We found that square images take up more screen real estate on mobile. So if you're running an ad, you want it to be a one to one ratio. Now when someone clicks this ad, which is talking about the product and the value of the product and how cool it is and how much people like it and how they have the opportunity to get it for free, when someone clicks that ad, it takes them over to a real simple opt-in page.

## [15:04 - 16:07]

as we have sales for our brand. This would run to the same people that you're running traditional paid traffic to to try to get people to buy your products.
